telltalePatterns: Realization of /ɹ/ as an alveolar tap [ɾ] (e.g., 'river', 'green')
Syllable-timed rhythm instead of stress-timed rhythm

prosody.fluencyNotes: Speech is fluent with appropriate pausing at clause boundaries, but lacks native-like connected speech features (e.g., linking, vowel reduction). Words tend to be pronounced distinctly.

strengths: Consistent aspiration of initial voiceless stops (e.g., 'park').

recommendedFocus.reason: These are high-frequency sounds in English. Correcting the substitution with /t/ and /d/ will have the largest single impact on reducing the foreign accent and improving clarity.

recommendedFocus.area: Vowel contrast /ɪ/ vs. /i/ (e.g., ship vs. sheep)
recommendedFocus.reason: This common vowel distinction is currently neutralized, which can lead to lexical confusion for listeners. Mastering this contrast is crucial for intelligibility.
Shifting from a syllable-timed to a stress-timed pattern by reducing unstressed vowels will dramatically improve the naturalness and flow of speech, making it easier for native listeners to process.

result.transcript: Yesterday the weather was really beautiful, so I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring is my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that, I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
